channel.title: PYRANA Daily
channel.description: Daily AI agent research, narrated. Two hosts unpack the day's most interesting papers from PYRANA Research Intelligence.
channel.itunes:author: Zeroth Agents

item.title: PYRANA Research Intelligence — April 27, 2026
item.description: Okay, here's the number that stopped me cold this morning. A new memory system called Memanto hits 89.8% on LongMemEval and 87.1% on LoCoMo — those are the two big long-horizon memory benchmarks — and it does it with a single retrieval query, sub-90 millisecond latency, and zero ingestion delay. No knowledge graph. No multi-query pipeline. Just a typed schema and a vector engine. Right, and the "n

item.title: PYRANA Research Intelligence — May 8, 2026
item.description: Okay, so imagine you're building what you call a superintelligent retrieval agent — and then a paper drops with literally that name. That's the energy we're starting with today. It's validating and a little terrifying at the same time. The paper scores an eight-point-oh-five on our priority index, which is the highest we've flagged in weeks, and the reason it hits that hard is that it's not just a

item.title: PYRANA Research Intelligence — April 29, 2026
item.description: Okay so imagine you're building a multi-agent framework, you've got your architecture mostly figured out, and then an open-source project drops that basically builds the same thing — but with one elegant design decision you haven't made yet. That's the OxyGent situation today, and we need to talk about it. Yeah OxyGent is the paper of the day for me, no question. Score of 8.05, high priority flag.

item.title: PYRANA Research Intelligence — April 28, 2026
item.description: Okay, picture this — a multi-agent system specifically designed to generate real-world evidence from clinical data, built on a standardized medical data model, and it scores an 8.4 out of 10 on our internal relevance tracker. That's our lead today, and honestly it might be the most practically important paper we've covered this month. FastOMOP. And for listeners who aren't deep in the pharma data

item.title: PYRANA Research Intelligence — April 30, 2026
item.description: Okay so imagine you're an AI agent, and every time you face a new situation, you can dynamically reach into a toolkit and assemble exactly the right skills on the fly. That's the core idea behind a paper called Bian Que, and honestly it might be the most practically useful thing we've read all week. Bian Que, named after the legendary Chinese physician by the way, and the metaphor actually works —
